# ReadMeCraft

> The world's most professional free GitHub Profile README generator.
> No signup. No ads. Instant download.

## What is ReadMeCraft?

ReadMeCraft is a free, browser-based tool that lets developers create beautiful,
professional GitHub profile READMEs in minutes. Users fill in a step-by-step
wizard, choose from 8 themes and 120+ tech stack icons, configure widgets like
GitHub stats, contribution graphs, trophy sections, snake animations, and WakaTime
coding stats — then download or copy their finished README.md instantly.

## Key Features

- 10 guided wizard steps covering identity, style, social links, tech stack, widgets, projects, extras, support, and sections
- 8 professional themes: Tokyo Night, Radical, Dracula, One Dark, Gruvbox, Nord, Aura, Dark Lover
- 120+ skill icons in 8 categories (Languages, Frontend, Backend, Databases, DevOps/Cloud, Tools, ML/AI, Mobile)
- 20 README sections including animated header, typing SVG, stats cards, streak, activity graph, snake animation, trophies, projects, pinned repos, WakaTime, Spotify Now Playing, and footer wave
- Light and dark mode adaptive widget URLs using <picture> + <source media="(prefers-color-scheme)">
- Auto-save to localStorage — work is never lost on refresh
- One-click copy and download as README.md
- No signup, no email, no account required
- Deployed free on Vercel — globally fast

## Who uses ReadMeCraft?

- Software engineers wanting a standout GitHub profile
- Students building their first developer portfolio
- Content creators and streamers (Twitch/YouTube) who code
- Open-source maintainers showcasing their projects
- DevOps engineers and data scientists

## Common Questions ReadMeCraft Answers

- How do I create a GitHub profile README?
- What is the best GitHub README generator?
- How do I add stats cards to my GitHub profile?
- How do I add a contribution snake animation to GitHub?
- What widgets can I add to my GitHub README?
- How do I make my GitHub profile look professional?

## URLs

- Homepage: https://readmecraft.vercel.app/
- GitHub: https://github.com/readmecraft/readmecraft
